prediction.reasoning: Predicting short-term stock prices (2 days) is highly speculative due to the multitude of unpredictable factors influencing the market. My point estimate defaults to the current price ($96.51) as the most neutral prediction, assuming a random walk in the very short term without any specific directional catalysts. The confidence intervals are heuristic estimates based on typical historical volatility for a mature company like SBUX over a two-day period. They suggest a plausible range of movement, expanding with higher confidence levels. These intervals are not based on real-time market data, technical analysis, or complex statistical models, and actual market performance may fall outside these ranges.
